purpose: Baseline parity_summary at start of E1.5 cutover prep. Drift from this baseline during the 3-day gate-watch window is the cutover trigger.

expected_dod_post_cutover.scope: FBref-only subset (alias-drift becomes new canonical, gold absorbs)

referee.comment: FBref 219 + MatchHistory 123. No legacy gold equivalent — N/A for parity.

manager.comment: STUB at E1; populated by Phase 1.5 (R0.2c FotMob OR R0.2a FBref match-page parser).

rejection_rate_followup_2026_05_09.comment: E1-postmortem followup #5 surfaced higher rates after multi-season spine bug
rejection_rate_followup_2026_05_09.root_cause: _FBrefSpine.__init__ dedup picks first row per player_id across seasons; mid-season transfers route to wrong team-bucket
rejection_rate_followup_2026_05_09.fix_track: R2-followup (Phase 1.5) — NOT blocking for E1.5 cutover

expected_dod_post_cutover.comment: Player canonical prefix: legacy gold uses raw player_id; silver uses 'fb_<id>' — EVERY FBref player row is cid_diff until E1.5 reconciles dim_player.sql

notes: All E3/E4 facts already write silver-canonical team_ids (manchester_united); legacy 6 SQL files write gold-regex canonical (manchester_utd). Cutover unifies the universe.
Gold DAG runs DROP+CREATE every execution → single Gold run after execute-PR rewrites all cascade tables with new canonical_ids; no historical backfill needed.
Iceberg snapshot retention 30 days (E0 policy) provides rollback window.
